records.ety: [L. dissensio: cf. F. dissension. See Dissent.]

defs: Disagreement in opinion, usually of a violent character, producing warm debates or angry words; contention in words; partisan and contentious divisions; breach of friendship and union; strife; discord; quarrel.
quotes: Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and disputation with them.
Debates, dissension, uproars are thy joy.
A seditious person and raiser-up of dissension among the people.
